call_transcript:
Agent: Hi Eleanor, this is Mark calling from Finance Solutions. I see you recently filled out a form online regarding a Personal Loan. Um, how are you doing today? 
Eleanor: Good, thanks. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $10,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 8.99%.  That's right, 8.99%.  Now, what were you hoping to use the funds for, if you don't mind me asking? 
Eleanor: Just some home improvements, you know, a new kitchen countertop. 
Agent: Excellent! A kitchen upgrade is a fantastic investment.  Now, with this loan, you'd have fixed monthly payments over, say, a five-year term.  We can adjust that, of course.  The total cost, including interest, would be… let me just calculate… around $12,000. Does that sound manageable? 
Eleanor: Hmm, that's… okay. What are the fees like? 
Agent: There's a small origination fee of $150, and that's it, really. No prepayment penalties.  We pride ourselves on transparency.  
Eleanor: Okay. And the monthly payment? 
Agent: Approximately $198 a month.  We can send you a full amortization schedule outlining everything.  
Eleanor: Yeah, that would be good. 
Agent: Wonderful! So, to proceed, I just need to verify a few details… your address is still Denver, Colorado, correct? 
Eleanor: Yes. 
Agent: Perfect. And your date of birth? 
Eleanor: (Provides DOB) 
Agent: Fantastic.  Okay, Eleanor, I'm going to go ahead and process this for you. You'll receive an email with the loan agreement within the hour.  
Eleanor: Great, thank you! 
Agent: You're very welcome! Have a wonderful day.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Miller? This is Sarah calling from Financial Freedom. We're offering a special promotion on our Signature Credit Card. 
Robert: Where did you get my number? I didn't ask for this. 
Agent: Sir, we… uh… we acquired your information through a marketing partner. We're pre-approving you for a $5,000 Signature Credit Card at 19.99%. 
Robert: 19.99?! Are you kidding me? That's outrageous! I'm already getting calls from you people. 
Agent: Sir, I assure you… 
Robert: No, no, no. I'm not interested. Don't call me again. (Click)

Agent: Hi Jessica, this is David from Secure Finance. I'm calling regarding a Personal Loan. Your friend, Michael, referred you. 
Jessica: Oh, hi! Yeah, Michael mentioned you guys. 
Agent: Great! So, we're pre-approving you for a $15,000 Personal Loan at a rate of 7.49%.  We can definitely work with you. What are you planning to use the funds for? 
Jessica: I'm thinking of consolidating some student loan debt. It's just… a lot. 
Agent: Absolutely. Debt consolidation is a smart move.  With our loan, you'd have one fixed monthly payment, potentially at a lower interest rate than your current loans.  
Jessica: I've been looking at a few options, actually.  Discover and SoFi both sent me offers. 
Agent: That's good you're shopping around!  However, our rate of 7.49% is very competitive, especially considering your excellent credit score. Discover is usually around 8.5%, and SoFi… well, they often have variable rates, which can be risky.  We offer fixed rates for the life of the loan. 
Jessica: Hmm, that's true about the variable rates. What's the loan term? 
Agent: We offer terms from 3 to 7 years.  A 5-year term would give you a monthly payment of around $290. 
Jessica: Okay. And are there any prepayment penalties? 
Agent: None whatsoever. You can pay it off early without any extra charges.  We want to make this as flexible as possible for you. 
Jessica: That's good to know. I need to think about it a little more. 
Agent: Of course! I can email you a detailed breakdown of the loan terms and a comparison chart with Discover and SoFi. Would that be helpful? 
Jessica: Yes, please! That would be great. 
Agent: Wonderful. I'll send that over right away.  Feel free to reach out if you have any questions.

Agent: Hello, Mr. Rodriguez? This is Lisa from Premier Financial. I see you applied for our Signature Credit Card online. 
David: Yeah, hi. I was just checking out my options. 
Agent: Excellent! We're pre-approving you for a $2,500 Signature Credit Card with a credit limit of $2,500 at an interest rate of 16.99%. 
David: 16.99%? That seems a little high. 
Agent: I understand your concern, sir. However, given your self-employment status and credit score, this is a very competitive rate. We can offer you rewards points on every purchase, and a low introductory APR for the first six months. 
David: Hmm… I'm not sure. I'm trying to build my credit. 
Agent: This card is actually a great way to do that! Responsible use will definitely improve your score. We report to all three major credit bureaus. 
David: Okay. What's the annual fee? 
Agent: There's a $99 annual fee, but you'll earn enough rewards points to more than offset that, especially if you use the card for everyday purchases. 
David: I see. Can you send me some more information about the rewards program? 
Agent: Absolutely! I'll email you a brochure detailing all the benefits. Would you like me to proceed with the application? 
David: Maybe. Send me the brochure first. 
Agent: Certainly. I'll send it over right now. Thank you for your time, Mr. Rodriguez.

Agent: Hello, Mrs. Hayes? This is Kevin from Universal Lending. We're offering a special… 
Patricia: (Interrupting) Oh, for goodness sake! You people called me *yesterday*! I told you I'm not interested! 
Agent: Ma'am, I apologize for the inconvenience. We're pre-approving you for a $3,000 Personal Loan at 24.99%… 
Patricia: 24.99?! That's highway robbery! I want my number removed from your list! I'm reporting you! (Click)
